description: Before a shared meal, someone gathers the ingredients and clears the table. 'thilonu' arranges a person, material, or condition for an expected later event. 'winora' (plan) organises intended actions, while preparation carries out the readying that the plan may call for.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced dental breath pass from 'th' into high-front unrounded 'i'. In middle 'lo', touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge, release voice along the sides, and round into stressed back 'o'. Final 'nu' closes at the dental contact with nasal voicing, then raises the tongue back and rounds the lips for high 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Breath, lateral flow, and nasal resonance arrive in a measured sequence. The orderly movement through three contacts can help the word recall things set in place before an event.
usage_notes: The person or thing made ready is the direct object. A 'lila' clause can give the later purpose, and reflexive 'miso' makes preparation of oneself explicit. As a noun, the same form is preparation.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared projects become workable when materials, access, and time are arranged before people are asked to act. Preparation makes those conditions part of the work.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Food kept for winter and tools put in order before work make preparation visible as ordinary care for what comes next.

semantic_domains.activity: practical readying carried out before a later event
semantic_domains.cognition: forethought applied to the conditions an activity will need
semantic_domains.temporal: an earlier action arranged in relation to what is expected later
